name: MetLife Stadium
tagline: This is where it all ends: the first-ever 48-team World Cup Final lands at the biggest stadium of the tournament.

why.title: Hosts the Final
why.text: MetLife stages the July 19 World Cup Final, the showpiece match of the entire tournament. The road to the trophy ends right here.

why.title: Biggest in the Cup
why.text: At ~80,800 seats it's the largest venue at WC2026, and the largest stadium in the NFL. Size is exactly why it landed the final.

why.title: Turned Turf to Grass
why.text: Crews buried the artificial turf under two feet of sand and stitched in a hybrid Bermudagrass pitch built to survive eight matches in summer heat.

why.title: Proven Big-Match Stage
why.text: It already hosted the 2016 Copa America Centenario final and the 2025 Club World Cup final. This building knows how to throw a championship.

gettingThere: Take the NJ Transit train, not a car. From Manhattan, ride from Penn Station to Secaucus Junction, then hop the Meadowlands shuttle that drops you at the stadium's front door in about 10 minutes.
transit: Meadowlands Rail Line runs straight to the stadium for every WC match; transfer at Secaucus Junction (marked 'SEC').
Penn Station NYC to Secaucus to stadium runs ~45-60 min, ~$12-15 each way.
Coach USA 351 'Meadowlands Express' bus runs from Port Authority to Lot K, starting ~2.5 hours before kickoff.
Driving is the backup, not the plan: event parking ~$40-60 and final-day lots already sold out.
matchday: There's no real neighborhood around the stadium, so the party lives across the river: FIFA's official NY/NJ Fan Festival, a converted mini-pitch at Rockefeller Center (Jul 6-19), and a free fan hub at Sports Illustrated Stadium in Harrison. Fans pre-game in NYC, Hoboken and Jersey City bars, then funnel onto the trains.
food: Jones Wood Foundry (Upper East Side): British gastropub and the prime England-supporters spot.
Smithfield Hall (Chelsea): a soccer-bar institution home to eight European supporters' clubs.
Football Factory at Legends (Koreatown): three levels of soccer-only chaos for big matches.
Hudson Hall (Jersey City): a big Eastern-European beerhall that takes reservations, ideal for groups on the NJ side.

food.name: Pasta Fresca by Nonna Fusco's
why: Lumache in spicy vodka sauce, fresh-made
Chicken cutlet add-on, real sit-down pasta
food.note: Lumache pasta, spicy vodka sauce, chicken cutlet add-on

why: Chicken or lamb over rice, NYC-cart classic
Gyros and pakora chips, big halal portions
food.note: Chicken or lamb over rice, gyros, pakora chips

food.note: Thumann's natural-casing all-beef Jersey deep-fried dogs

food.name: Craft Beer Hall
why: 40-plus rotating taps in one spot
Strong regional NJ brewery lineup
food.note: 40-plus rotating taps, strong regional NJ brewery lineup

pre.name: NYNJ World Cup 26 Jersey Fan Hub (Sports Illustrated Stadium)
pre.where: Harrison, NJ; PATH to Harrison station, ~30 min from stadium via Secaucus
why: Official free fan zone, replaced cancelled Liberty State Park site
60-ft pitch screen, all 104 matches, PATH-direct

pre.name: Bolero Snort Brewery
why: Closest real brewery to the gates
Local NJ taproom, fresh pours pre-match
pre.note: Local taproom, closest real brewery to the gates

Walkable-close, friendly pre-game energy
pre.note: Irish pub, excellent Guinness, friendly matchday crowd

Cold beer, old-school Jersey institution
pre.note: 1936 roadhouse, sizzling platters and cold beer

pre.name: Marcus Live! Bar & Grille

Big screens, bridge-walk to the gates
pre.note: Samuelsson's gameday wings, smash burgers, big screens

post.note: 50-plus TVs, Hudson views, big sound system

post.note: Outdoor watch party, food trucks, skyline backdrop

merch.name: MetLife Stadium Flagship Team Store
merch.where: Main concourse, MetLife Stadium (Giants/Jets official store)

merch.name: adidas Soccer Flagship Store
merch.where: American Dream, Court D, Level 2, East Rutherford (first US soccer-only adidas flagship, opened Feb 2026, by the pedestrian bridge)

merch.name: American Dream World Cup Vendor Village
merch.where: Inside American Dream, steps from the pedestrian bridge to the stadium

tips: Beat the post-match train crush: either sprint to the platform the instant the whistle blows, or linger 30-45 min to let the first wave clear. The slow middle is the worst.
Load your return fare BEFORE the match so you're not stuck in a ticket line with 80,000 people.
Arrive 2-3 hours early for the final: airport-style security on 80k fans and a tighter perimeter make gates brutal.
Clear-bag policy is strict: clear plastic bag max 12x6x12, a gallon Ziploc, or a tiny clutch. No backpacks.
For the 3 PM July final, the west-facing upper deck bakes in full sun; aim for east-side or lower-bowl shade if heat-sensitive.
Base in Hoboken or Jersey City for cheaper rooms and a shorter transit hop than Manhattan.
weather: Expect a hot, humid mid-summer afternoon (June ~77F, July final ~87F) in full open-air sun, so pack sunscreen, a hat, and a sealed clear water bottle under 20 oz.
lore: Copa America Centenario Final 2016: Chile beat Argentina on penalties, the biggest soccer final here before the World Cup.
Hosted Super Bowl XLVIII (2014), the first cold-weather Super Bowl in the NY/NJ area.
Staged the 2025 FIFA Club World Cup Final, the venue's full dress rehearsal for the big one.
A top-tier concert and WrestleMania stage (WrestleMania 29 and 35), built for ~$1.6B as the priciest US stadium ever at completion.

transport.summary: This is a transit-only World Cup: there is no general parking, so the NJ Transit train via Secaucus Junction is the move. A match ticket is required to buy any official transport, and everything must be pre-purchased.

rail.name: NJ Transit Meadowlands Rail (World Cup special)
rail.station: Meadowlands Sports Complex station, at the stadium's front door (~5 min walk to the MetLife/Verizon gates)
rail.detail: From NYC Penn Station, ride to Secaucus Junction then take the matchday-only Meadowlands shuttle train (~10 min) to the stadium. Service starts a few hours before kickoff and runs a couple hours post-match; capped at 40,000 riders per match, so it sells out. Allow 60-90 min Midtown-to-seat. Buy on the NJ Transit app only (njtworldcup.com); ticket holders only.
rail.deal: $98 round trip (a World Cup special; normal Meadowlands fare is ~$12.90)

bus.name: Official NYNJ Stadium Shuttle (FIFA)
bus.from: Four hubs: Midtown North (Red), Midtown East (Green), Port Authority Bus Terminal (Blue), Hackensack Meridian School of Medicine (Yellow)
bus.detail: Direct round-trip coach service to the stadium, ticket-holders only, non-transferable, accessible options available. Advance purchase via fevo.com/official-nynj-stadium-shuttles.

points.t: round trip from four NYC/NJ hubs

bus.deal: NYC bus slashed to $20 round trip

bus.name: Coach USA 351 Meadowlands Express
bus.from: Port Authority Bus Terminal, South Wing 3rd floor (Gates 411-414)
bus.detail: Standard MetLife event bus to Lot K near the Quest Diagnostics Performance Center (~20 min). Service begins ~2.5-3 hrs before the event; last bus ~1 hr after it ends. Buy online for priority boarding at 351express.com.

shuttle.name: NYNJ Stadium Shuttle (color lines)
shuttle.detail: FIFA's official park-and-ride/express coach network. Catch the Red, Green, Blue or Yellow line at its named hub; round trip, pre-booked, ~$20 from NYC. Same system as the bus above.

points.t: Red, Green, Blue, Yellow at named hubs
round trip, same system as bus

transport.fromAirport: Newark Liberty (EWR) is closest and in NJ; from EWR take NJ Transit to Secaucus Junction, then the matchday Meadowlands train. From JFK/LGA, get into Manhattan (NYC Penn or Port Authority) and take the rail or official shuttle from there.
transport.rideshare: Uber/Lyft drop-off and pickup are NOT allowed on stadium property. The designated rideshare zone is at Meadowlands Racing and Entertainment, ~1.3 miles (2.1 km) from the gates via a marked walking path. Expect heavy surge pricing and long post-match waits.
tips: You must hold a valid match ticket to buy ANY official transport (rail, shuttle, parking) and tickets are non-transferable and checked before boarding.
Rail is capped at 40,000 per match and sells out; book the NJ Transit app ticket the moment it opens and have your return loaded before kickoff.
Post-match return at Secaucus is the #1 bottleneck for 80,000 fans; either sprint to the platform on the final whistle or linger 30-45 min to let the first wave clear.
Don't try to walk in from NYC or nearby roads, they're highways with no pedestrian access.

parking.summary: MetLife is transit-only for the World Cup: there is NO general spectator parking and NO tailgating at any match, including the Final. The only car option is a pricey pre-booked off-site space.

lots.name: American Dream Mall (off-site)
lots.price: $225 per match day (dynamic pricing, prepaid only)
lots.detail: Several thousand spaces connected to the stadium by two pedestrian bridges. Must be pre-purchased through JustPark, FIFA's official parking partner; no on-site or gate payment accepted.

points.t: two pedestrian bridges to the stadium

lots.name: Accessible J Lot (ADA)
lots.detail: ADA accessible parking passes for the stadium's J Lot are sold in advance through JustPark / the official FIFA site. Requires a valid match ticket.

points.t: for J Lot, sold via JustPark

parking.prepaid: All parking is prepaid only via JustPark (justpark.com), FIFA's official 2026 partner. No on-site payments are accepted; a valid match ticket is required to reserve.
parking.accessible: ADA accessible parking is in J Lot (prepaid via JustPark). For guests who don't need to park, a free ADA drop-off zone operates in Lot C near the Verizon Gate.
parking.tailgating: Tailgating is explicitly banned at all World Cup matches, a sharp break from the normal NFL Meadowlands tailgate scene.
tips: Skip driving entirely: with no general parking and no tailgating, the $98 train is cheaper and faster than the $225 American Dream space.
If you must drive, reserve American Dream on JustPark early, it's capped and surges in price.
Need an ADA drop-off but not parking? Use the free Lot C zone by the Verizon Gate.
Standard NFL Giants/Jets parking ($40-60, on-site, tailgating allowed) does NOT apply to the World Cup, don't assume the usual setup.
